2026-04-23T05:58:57.8764321Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8764903Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8765340Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8765723Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8766023Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8766257Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8766420Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8766943Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8767131Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8767362Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8767567Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8767861Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:58:57.8768112Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-23T06:02:36.0449978Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T06:00:14.348047547Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58553","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-23T06:02:36.0481598Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T06:00:14.737466763Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58553,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Update design.md for reachability-based model filtering and shared resource model handling\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (generator)\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #58482) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58482 introduced two significant behavioral changes to the provisioning generator:\\n\\n1. **Reachability-based model/enum filtering** — `ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks resource models\\u0026#39; property graphs recursively (`CollectReachableTypes()`) and only passes reachable `InputModelType`/`InputEnumType` instances to the type factory. This replaces the old approach of emitting all input models/enums and relying on the post-processor to prune unreferenced types.\\n\\n2. **Shared resource model handling** — `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore` now handles the case where one `InputModelType` backs multiple `ProvisioningResourceProvider` instances. It selects the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model\\u0026#39;s own name (alphabetically first as a tiebreaker), ensuring flattened properties are forwarded correctly.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`design.md` § 6 \\u0026#34;Output Library\\u0026#34;** currently says: *\\u0026#34;Only resources are marked as \\u0026#39;types to keep\\u0026#39; — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums.\\u0026#34;*  This is no longer accurate. The filtering now happens up-front via `CollectReachableTypes()` in `ProvisioningOutputLibrary`, not via post-processor pruning. The section must be updated to describe the reachability walk (base/derived models, properties, array/dictionary/union element types) and explain why it is needed (avoid dead types like `XxxListResult` paging envelopes, patch wrappers, and error models).\\n\\n- **`design.md` § 5 \\u0026#34;TypeFactory \\u0026amp; Type Resolution\\u0026#34;** (or § 6 \\u0026#34;Resource Provider\\u0026#34;) has no mention of the shared resource model scenario where a single `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider`. The canonical provider selection logic and why it is necessary (so `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` on the correct parent resource) is undocumented.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n**Change 1 — Replace the \\u0026#34;Output Library\\u0026#34; paragraph (§ 6)**\\n\\nLocate this text:\\n````\\nThe output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead iterates input models and enums directly, routing each through our TypeFactory. Only resources are marked as \\u0026#34;types to keep\\u0026#34; — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums.\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n```\\nThe output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead builds the set of models and enums to emit explicitly via a **reachability walk** (`CollectReachableTypes`). Starting from every resource model registered in `ArmProviderSchema.Resources`, it recursively traverses base/derived types, all property types, and element types of array, dictionary, and union types. Only models and enums reachable through this walk are passed to the type factory.\\n\\nThis avoids emitting dead types that have no place in a provisioning library — for example, paging envelope types (`XxxListResult`), patch/update wrappers, and generic error models. These types appear in the full input model graph but are never referenced from a resource\\u0026#39;s property surface.\\n````\\n\\n**Change 2 — Add a \\u0026#34;Shared Resource Models\\u0026#34; subsection**\\n\\nIn § 5 \\u0026#34;TypeFactory \\u0026amp; Type Resolution\\u0026#34;, after the existing \\u0026#34;Resource Detection\\u0026#34; subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Shared Resource Models\\n\\nIn rare cases the same `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider` (for example, when `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` creates a virtual sub-resource whose payload reuses the parent resource\\u0026#39;s model). `CreateModelCore` must return a single `ModelProvider` for each input model.\\n\\nWhen the output library records multiple providers for one model, the type factory selects the **canonical provider** — the one whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model\\u0026#39;s own name. If no exact match exists, it falls back to the alphabetically first resource name. This ensures the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` attaches flattened forwarding properties to the parent resource (e.g., `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` / `SkuName`), while the sub-resource (e.g., `ProfileRevision`) only exposes the properties it needs.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippets are required for this change — the affected file is an internal design document, not a user-facing README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe file being updated (`docs/design.md`) is an internal design document, not a user-facing package README. Standard README section validation does not apply.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThis is a documentation-only change (Markdown). No build or test steps are required. After editing:\\n\\n1. Confirm the Markdown renders correctly (no broken headings, fenced blocks, or list formatting).\\n2. Optionally run a Markdown linter if one is configured for the repo.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24819209510/agentic_workflow) · ● 933.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58553\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:57:10Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T05:57:10Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-23T06:02:36.0613680Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T06:00:39.524961083Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":3292095671,\"number\":51705,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable\\u003cCosmosDBAccountCapability\\u003e\",\"body\":\"## Background\\nThis is a \\\"sub-issue\\\" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).\\n\\n## Request\\nIt would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable\\u003cCosmosDBAccountCapability\\u003e`. It would make the \\\"upserting\\\" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property 2026-04-23T06:02:36.0682111Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T06:00:39.542068597Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":24,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4222840604,\"number\":57921,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nCommit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now:\\n\\n- **If the default API version is GA** → only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded)\\n- **If the default API version is itself a preview** → all versions (GA + preview) are listed\\n\\nThe generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` — The \\\"ResourceVersions Nested Class\\\" section says *\\\"Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order\\\"* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview.\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` — Section 6 \\\"Output Types\\\" describes `ResourceVersions` only as *\\\"nested class with GA API version constants\\\"*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included.\\n- Neither document explains what constitutes a \\\"preview\\\" version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic — version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.).\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`\\n\\nEdit the \\\"ResourceVersions Nested Class\\\" section. Locate the paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\nLists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order:\\n```\\n\\nReplace it with:\\n\\n```markdown\\nLists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource:\\n\\n- **When the default version is GA** — only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out.\\n- **When the default version is itself a preview** — all versions (GA and preview) are included.\\n\\nA version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive).\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md`\\n\\nIn Section 6 \\\"Output Types\\\" → \\\"Resource Provider\\\" bullet list, locate:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThese are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check).\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits.\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e
